title: Digital payment methods
description: Practice understanding of digital wallets, payment apps, Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) and understanding which of them are loans.

assessment_items.question_content:
You use this app to scan and upload all your credit and debit cards so you no longer have to carry them with you.

**What does this describe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a digital wallet (like Apple Pay)
a payment app (like Venmo)
Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) service (like Affirm)
a credit card

assessment_items.question_content:
You use this payment method by tapping your phone on a payment terminal.

**What does this describe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: a person holding a cell phone over a payment terminal. Payment terminal is an electronic that has a small screen and numbered buttons.  A cup of coffee is nearby.
options.caption: Payment terminal. *Image credit: Mobile payment by HLundgaard, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0), via Wikimedia Commons*

choices.content: a digital wallet (like Apple Pay)
a payment app (like Venmo)
Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) service (like Affirm)
a credit card

assessment_items.question_content:
Activating a digital wallet app on your smartphone has many benefits.

**What is one of these benefits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It lets you pay for purchases over time.
It safely carries all your cards without needing the physical ones.
It directly transfers money to other users through an app.
It functions like a traditional debit card for withdrawals.

assessment_items.question_content:
You use this method to quickly send money to friends or pay for services directly from your phone.

**What does this describe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a digital wallet (like Apple Pay)
a payment app (like CashApp)
Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) service (like Klarna)
a credit card

assessment_items.question_content:
You're having a garage sale and need a quick way to accept payments besides cash since some people might not carry any.

**What payment method could you use?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: digital wallet
payment app (like Venmo or PayPal)
gift card
Buy Now Pay Now (like After pay)

assessment_items.question_content:
Using a payment app on your smartphone has benefits.

**What is one of these benefits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It allows you to make immediate money transfers to others.
It securely stores all your credit and debit cards in one place.
It lets you pay for purchases over time without interest.
It provides special offers and discounts from retailers.

assessment_items.question_content:
You're ordering a replacement part online, and see the following:

Price | You save | Now | or pay:
-------------|------------|------------------------
$\cancel{\$249.99}$  | $\$50.00$ off  | $\$199.99$|  as low as **$\$22$/mo** with EZPay 

**What does choosing the $\$22$/month with EZ Pay option mean?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You must use a digital wallet to complete the purchase.
You sign up for a loan.
You pay using a payment app for instant transfers.
You are getting a special discount.

assessment_items.question_content:
Having a digital wallet means you don't have to carry any debit or credit cards with you. You pay by using your phone instead.

**What would be a drawback to using a digital wallet?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You must have a secure internet connection to use it.
The money in digital wallet is not insured.
Losing your phone means temporarily losing access to all your payment methods.
Digital wallets only work on Android phones.

assessment_items.question_content:
You used a money app to buy concert tickets from a person. The seller never sent you the tickets and has stopped responding.

**What are your options for getting the money back?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: refund from the money app
no options are available
contact the stranger
call your bank

assessment_items.question_content:
The money app you normally use goes out of business and you can no longer log into it. You had $\$100$ in the app.

**Who covers this loss?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC)
National Credit Union Association (NCUA)
phone insurance
no insurance is available

assessment_items.question_content:
The local appliance store is offering you the option to pay for a refrigerator in four monthly payments, with no interest, through a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) service. You are already making payments on a newer car. 

**What effect could this payment program have on your finances?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: This added debt could make it harder to make your car payments.
There will no effect to your finances.
You will have paid more than the original cost of the refrigerator.
Your car payments will increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
As a future home owner, you first need to apply for a home loan at a bank. Part of that application involves pulling your credit score. Three months ago you bought at TV on a Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) plan.

**How did this BNPL purchase affect your credit score?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It decreased your credit score.
It raised your credit score.
Nothing happened to your credit score.
It restarted your credit score.
